title: Persistent Connections, Pipelining, Ordering, Reuse, and Closure · Go Semantics Case: Request Body Before Next
summary: A server dispatches the next request before consuming the current body, writes responses out of order, reuses connections after framing errors, and waits forever for idle peers. Planned artifact: Add and verify the request body before next increment in persistent-connection request and response state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Consume, reject-and-close, or deliberately drain the current framed request before parsing a subsequent request.
Determine HTTP/1.0 and HTTP/1.1 persistence from version, Connection options, framing completeness, errors, and local policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Request Body Before Next · Theory Request Body Before Next
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 2247 in a fuzzing harness varying every legal input segmentation. Build deterministic Go evidence to preserve HTTP/1.1 response order and reject reuse after incomplete or ambiguous input; preserve this operating constraint: one connection has exactly one read owner and one terminal close owner; then replace one field with repeated non-combinable lines and preserve their order. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Consume, reject-and-close, or deliberately drain the current framed request before parsing a subsequent request.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Request Body Before Next contract for the supplied data.

steps.title: Build Request Body Before Next evidence · Theory Request Body Before Next

steps.title: Repair the Request Body Before Next defect · Theory Request Body Before Next
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 2252 in a incident replay lab retaining minimized request-smuggling corpora. Build deterministic Go evidence to preserve HTTP/1.1 response order and reject reuse after incomplete or ambiguous input; preserve this operating constraint: one connection has exactly one read owner and one terminal close owner; then inject Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length and close before any downstream forwarding. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Consume, reject-and-close, or deliberately drain the current framed request before parsing a subsequent request.
